title: The Parameter Set (120,12,12,0) for Balanced Splittable Hadamard Matrices
micro_paper_assessment: Strong structural slice only. The forced 12K10 and 5+/5- block-zero-sum reduction is theorem-facing, but there is no exact existence verdict yet, so the current packet is not paper-ready.
family_affinity: high; the same B^2=nB reduction extends across the Type 2 family BSHM(8rs,4s,4s,0)
generalization_signal: The selected-row column graph is forced to be 4s disjoint cliques K_{2r}; equivalently H1 is column-permutation equivalent to H_(4s) times the all-ones row on 2r columns.
candidate_theorem_slice: Any BSHM(120,12,12,0), if it exists, has selected 12-row submatrix H_12 times the all-ones row on 10 columns up to column permutation, and every complementary row has a 5+/5- split on each ten-column block.
next_action: verify the 12K10 reduction carefully, then decide whether the complementary 108-row block model can support a bounded exact obstruction or extension argument without generic search
